MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-06-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jon C. James of HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing through CourtCall for Intervenor and moving party Landmark Insurance Company.
Andrew Chew of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing through CourtCall for plaintiff and opposing party Michael Sarao.

RULING:
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Intervenor Landmark Insurance Company's Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden via the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 855. Defendant failed to address Plaintiff's deposition testimony identifying Joe Ubeda, Fred Sherwood, and John De La Rosa as co-worker witnesses with knowledge. (Declaration of Jason Rose [Exhibit A at 83:20-84:8; 86:9-86:24].) Defendant failed to question Plaintiff about his exposure to asbestos from the installation of new gaskets and removal of old gaskets. Defendant's inability to name the brand name, manufacturer, or supplier of old gaskets is insufficient to shift the burden. (Declaration of Jon James [Exhibit G at 56:24-57:13; 57:24-58:5; 60:3-11; 60:23-61:5]; Declaration of Jason Rose [Exhibit A at 63:18-22; 64:10-25; 69:13-23].) Further, Defendant failed to address Plaintiff's testimony that the insulation pipe that was removed contained asbestos. (Declaration of Jon James [Exhibit G at 76:24-77:22; 78:1-10; 78:19-79:10].) Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

Intervenor Landmark Insurance Company As Alleged Insurer Of, Certain Underwriters At Lloyd'S London, Certain London Market Insurance Companies, Landmark Insurance Company, Motion For Summary Judgment (Added To Calendar For Issuance Of Tentative Ruling Only)
Off Calendar Feb-04-2021 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-04-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jon C. James of HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing through CourtCall for Intervenors and moving paries National Union Fire Insurance Company of Pittsburgh, PA, TIG Insurance Company, Certain Underwriters at Lloyd's London, and Certain London Market Insurance Companies.
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing through CourtCall for plaintiff and opposing party Michael Sarao.

RULING:
Not contested and tentative ruling adopted: Intervenors National Union Fire Insurance Company of Pittsburgh, PA, TIG Insurance Company, Certain Underwriters at Lloyd's London, and Certain London Market Insurance Companies' Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. Intervenors National Union Fire Insurance Company of Pittsburgh, PA, TIG Insurance Company, Certain Underwriters at Lloyd's London, and Certain London Market Insurance Companies ("Intervenors") have failed to sustain their initial burden by making an affirmative showing that Plaintiff does not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, needed evidence to support the element of causation. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 854-855.) Initially, it is undisputed between the parties that Plaintiff was present while J&H Marine & Industrial Engineering Company employees ("J&H employees") removed and installed alleged asbestos-containing flooring material upon five (5) ships between 1981-1985. (Intervenors' Statement of Undisputed Material Facts, No. 8-9.) Further, Plaintiff's responses to Intervenors' Special Interrogatories include specific facts about the way in which Plaintiff was exposed to asbestos while he was present and witnessed the activities of J&H employees aboard the five (5) ships identified. While Intervenors' argue that Plaintiff does not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, evidence proving the materials identified contained asbestos, this argument is not supported by the evidence submitted along with Intervenors' Motion. While Intervenors rely on Plaintiff's deposition testimony wherein he states that he does not know whether the materials used or removed by J&H employees contained asbestos, it cannot be inferred from Plaintiff's own ignorance that Plaintiff will be unable to produce other evidence or testimony proving asbestos-content. (See Weber v. John Crane, Inc. (2006) 143 Cal.App.4th 1433, 1439.) Further, while Intervenors argue Plaintiff's document production is factually devoid, Intervenors fail to elaborate. Intervenors do not refer to specific documents produced by Plaintiff and do not explain how the documents produced by Plaintiff support the contention that Plaintiff does not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, evidence proving the element of causation. Lastly, Intervenors reliance on Smith v. AC and S (1994) 31 Cal.App.4th 77 (Smith) and Chaknova v. Wilbur-Ellis (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 962 (Chaknova) is unavailing. In Smith, the Court of Appeal noted that in order to prove causation with respect to asbestos-exposure, "there must be proof that the defendant's asbestos products or activities were present at plaintiff's worksite." (Smith v. AC and S (1994) 31 Cal.App.4th 77, 89.) As noted above, it is undisputed between the parties that Plaintiff was present while J&H employees removed and installed alleged asbestos-containing flooring material upon five (5) ships between 1981-1985. (Intervenors' Statement of Undisputed Material Facts, No. 8-9.) Further, the reasoning employed in Chaknova has been expressly disapproved by the Supreme Court. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co., supra, 25 Cal.4th at p. 854-855, fn. 23; Weber v. John Crane, supra, 143 Cal.App.4th at p. 1441.) Accordingly, Intervenors' Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File An Answer In Intervention
Off Calendar Aug-20-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing by CourtCall for plaintiff Ronald Brown.

RULING: PROPOSED INTERVENOR LANDMARK INSURANCE COMPANY, AS ALLEGED INSURER OF ROBERT E. BLAKE D/B/A GENERAL ENGINEERING & MACHINE, A DISSOLVED CALIFORNIA CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE AN ANSWER IN INTERVENTION.

No argument is held. Ms. Chu requests that the Court allow a modification to its tentative ruling to allow plaintiff to add language that the denial as to plaintiff's request that they be reimbursed any duplicative discovery costs be denied "without prejudice" so that if plaintiff wishes that they may pursue those costs in a separate motion.

The Court granted Ms. Chu's request and adopted the tentative ruling as follows with the modification as requested: Proposed Intervenor Landmark Insurance Company's Motion for Leave to File an Answer in Intervention is GRANTED. Landmark must file its Answer in Intervention within 10 days of the entry of this order. Plaintiff's request that Landmark be responsible, and reimburse Plaintiff, for any duplicative discovery costs incurred by Plaintiff as a result of Landmark's late appearance in this action is denied. Plaintiff offers no authority for the proposition that intervenors are required to provide the requested disclosures. Plaintiff is to prepare a form of order incorporationg the modification. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: J. Alameda. Matter not reported. (503/CML)
